behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator -> row-index tracking -> DLQ writer handoff, not a single pure function (MOCK-MISS). No real OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce this specific behavior if DLQ is exercised via in-process wiring (REAL-INFRA no). No AI/ML dependency (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires rows to appear in DLQ with row number, not real queue service/network semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline modules together against a small CSV fixture and an in-process DLQ sink; assert rows missing required fields are excluded from normalized output and emitted to DLQ with exact original CSV row numbers.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires cross-module state handoff (parser/validator/DLQ), so Integration is necessary.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is transformation logic for date parsing/normalization in one function/object boundary (MOCK-MISS indicates local algorithm). No real infra needed (REAL-INFRA no). No AI/ML dependency (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher level: behavior text is only about format conversion correctness, not module handoff or external systems.
behaviors.test_description: Call the date-normalization function with representative ISO 8601, US, and European date strings (including edge cases like ambiguous day/month) and assert canonical output or explicit rejection for invalid/ambiguous inputs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Assertions target pure parsing/format logic; higher-level wiring is unnecessary.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on runtime memory pressure, timeout behavior, and process-level performance characteristics (REAL-INFRA yes: OS/process resources). In-process unit/integration doubles can hide allocation patterns and GC behavior (MOCK-HIDE yes). No AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real configured timeout/resource limits; measure completion time and peak memory, and fail if timeout exceeded or OOM occurs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly requires real process/OS resource behavior, matching System.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: This failure is at the real HTTP/S3 interaction boundary plus retry timing behavior (REAL-INFRA yes). Mocked clients often miss SDK/network semantics and can falsely pass backoff behavior (MOCK-HIDE yes). No AI/ML dependency. Rejected lower Integration: behavior text explicitly references S3 transient 503 from bucket, which is an external service interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against a test endpoint that returns real HTTP 503 for initial attempts then succeeds; assert retry count, increasing backoff intervals, and eventual batch continuation without full-batch failure.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real HTTP error/retry behavior, so System is required.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires restart semantics plus durable DB state/idempotency across process lifecycles (REAL-INFRA yes: subprocess/process restart + real DB effects). In-process mocks can hide transaction/checkpoint/commit ordering issues (MOCK-HIDE yes). No AI/ML dependency. Rejected lower Integration: behavior text explicitly includes 'restarted mid-batch,' which introduces process boundary effects.
behaviors.test_description: Process part of a batch into a real PostgreSQL instance, terminate/restart the pipeline process mid-batch, then complete processing and assert no duplicate rows (via unique keys/count checks) while preserving all expected successful rows.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Process restart and durable DB correctness require System-level execution.
